What we serve

The menu.

All house-made, fresh ingredients. We fry exclusively in beef tallow.

Bresee Pleasers
Donut Holes$1 each
Cinnamon sugar, maple bacon, powdered sugar. Coffee & donuts from 9am.
Fried Mozzarella$10
4 large slabs breaded fried mozzarella served with raspberry or marinara
Bresee Wings$12
9 traditional or 10 boneless in your choice of buffalo, BBQ, General Tso's, Hot Honey, Ranch or Hot Cheeto Dry Rub. Served with ranch, blue cheese, carrots or celery.
Hand Breaded Fried Pickles$10
House-made hand breaded fried pickles served with ranch
Bresee Demolition Nachos$15
Towering pile of chili or chicken nachos stacked high with cheddar jack, queso, lettuce, tomato, fresh jalapeño, sour cream and salsa. Big enough to share.
Deviled Eggs$1.50 each
You choose how many you want
Smash Burgers
Classic Build Your Own$13
Two patties, American cheese, lettuce, tomato, onion and pickle. Served with fries.
Gameday Burger$15
BBQ sauce, cheddar, crunchy fried onion and bacon. Served with fries.
Better Peanut Butter Burger$15
Bacon, cheddar, peanut butter and pickle. Served with fries.
⭐ Bresee Burger$15
Herbed cream cheese, bacon, house-made onion jam, topped with fried pickles. Served with fries.
Salads
House Salad$5
Mixed greens, tomato, cucumbers, onion & croutons
Buffalo Chicken Salad$14
Boneless wings tossed in signature sauce over mixed greens, carrots, celery. Served with bleu cheese or ranch. Substitute tofu available.
Bresee Mac Salad$13
Mixed greens, chopped onions, pickles, topped with American cheese & special sauce

Craft cocktails

Every drink tells
a Danville story.

Our cocktail menu is a love letter to the people and places that shaped this city — from Abraham Lincoln to Gene Hackman to Dick & Jerry Van Dyke.

Lincoln Mule
Tito's Vodka, raspberry syrup, lime juice, and ginger beer
Abraham Lincoln practiced law in Danville from 1841 to 1859. It was here that he made his final address in Illinois before departing for Washington on February 11, 1861.
Gin Hackman
Beefeater gin, vermouth, served ice cold with a lemon twist or olives
Gene Hackman, born in 1930, was a Danville native and acclaimed actor who won two Academy Awards, two BAFTAs, and four Golden Globes — considered one of the best actors of his generation.
The Van Dyke
Maker's Mark or El Bandido Yankee Añejo, sugar, bitters, stirred to perfection with an orange peel
Dick and Jerry Van Dyke, both Danville natives, brought fame to the city. Dick's lineage traces back to Mayflower passenger John Alden.
Sunset on Vermilion Margarita
El Bandido Yankee Tequila, blue curaçao, freshly squeezed lime juice, and a dash of simple syrup
Lake Vermilion is a 1,000-acre lake on the northwest side of Danville, open year-round and especially popular in summer with power boating, jet skiing, and fishing tournaments.
Bresee Mary
Tito's Vodka or El Bandido Yankee Tequila, bold Bloody Mary mix, finished with a chamoy rim
The Bresee Tower was constructed in 1918 as the First National Bank. The tower became a landmark and focal point for downtown Danville — and the site once housed an office where Abraham Lincoln practiced law.
The Walk-off
Spicy margarita with El Bandido Tequila blanco, fresh jalapeños and limes, rimed with Chamoy and tajin
Danville Stadium, home of the Danville Dans, is a ballpark built in 1946 as a Brooklyn Dodgers farm team location. It hosted Jackie Robinson and served as a filming location for The Babe.
